title: Syracuse residents urged to voice opinions on proposed $348M budget
summary: Syracuse Mayor Walsh proposed a historic $348M budget with a 2% tax and water rate increase, requiring $27M in reserve fund withdrawals. A public meeting on April 29 invited resident input before the Common Council considers amendments by May 7. City Auditor Alexander Marion issued a first-ever budget review, warning that the shrinking tax base cannot sustain the highest property tax in city history and cautioning about long-term fiscal sustainability if reserve depletion continues.

political_signal.description: City Auditor's unprecedented budget review signals governance oversight concern; councilor opposition to tax increase reflects constituent pressure and fiscal sustainability scrutiny; reserve fund depletion framed as unsustainable long-term strategy.

processing_notes: Dollar amounts: $348M (budget), $27M (reserve withdrawal); 2% increases (tax and water rates)
Key data quality note: Article contains publication date inconsistency (April 28 and April 29); public meeting date inferred as April 29, 2025
